title: viola ambigua subsp. thomasiana
intro: Viola ambigua subsp. thomasiana, also known as the Thomas' violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, and it has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and skin problems. The herbal preparations of Viola ambigua subsp. thomasiana include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
